title: RETHINKING LEASING RESTRICTIONS UNDER THE KERALA LAND REFORMS FRAMEWORK
description: The Kerala Land Reforms Act, 1963 (KLR Act) is widely regarded as one of India’s most progressive redistributive land reform legislations, having dismantled intermediary tenures and conferred ownership rights on cultivating tenants. While its equity objectives were historically transformative, the […] The post RETHINKING LEASING RESTRICTIONS UNDER THE KERALA LAND REFORMS FRAMEWORK appeared first on Centre for Public Policy Research (CPPR).
